title: digitalis × fulva
intro: Digitalis × fulva, also known as foxglove, is a biennial plant that is a hybrid of Digitalis purpurea and Digitalis grandiflora. The medicinal properties of Digitalis × fulva lie in its cardiac glycosides, which have been used to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of this plant include improved heart function, reduced symptoms of heart failure, and a decrease in the risk of death from heart-related complications. Digitalis × fulva can be prepared into herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and capsules, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
